challengeTitle: Admin Challenge Curation UI Prototype Challenge Overview Thi

categorizedRequirements.title: Enable Admin Tab with Role-Based Access Control
Add Development Mode for Admin Role Simulation
Create Admin Sidebar with Curation Filter Buttons
Implement Sort and Filter Panel in Content Window
Display Paginated Challenge List with Scrollable Area
Develop Challenge Card to Show Metadata and Curation Annotations
Implement Mock Challenge Service with Stubbed API Calls
Define TypeScript Interfaces for Challenge and Annotation Models
Implement SWR Hook for Challenge List Data Fetching
Implement Admin Role Check Hook
Structure Components and Files in rfp-tool Module
Update Constants with Admin-Specific Values

revisedScorecard.name: Revised Scorecard for Admin Tab and Development Mode Requirements

questions.description: The build configuration includes a MOCK_ADMIN_MODE environment variable or constant that enables the admin tab during development, bypassing actual role checks.
questions.guidelines: Yes: The environment variable or constant is defined and functional. No: Missing or non-functional development mode.

questions.description: Does the submission compile and build without errors?
questions.guidelines: Yes: Running pnpm build succeeds with zero errors and produces build output. No: Build command fails or produces errors.

questions.description: Are all required files included (source code, README, configuration)?
questions.guidelines: Yes: All deliverables listed in the spec are present in the submission. No: One or more required files are missing.

questions.description: Does the Admin Tab appear only when the user has the administrator role, as determined by useProfileContext() hook?
questions.guidelines: Yes: The tab is visible and functional only when the user has the administrator role. No: The tab appears regardless of role or is missing entirely.

questions.description: Does the UI correctly display a disabled or hidden state for the Admin Tab when the user does not have administrator role?
questions.guidelines: Yes: The tab is visually hidden or grayed out when the user lacks administrator role. No: The tab is visible or functional without proper role check.

questions.description: Does the AdminSidebar component include filter buttons for Curated A, Curated B, Curated C, Uncurated, and Discarded categories?
questions.guidelines: Yes: All five filter buttons are present and correctly labeled. No: One or more buttons are missing.

questions.description: Do filter buttons display active state with green highlight and challenge count badges (e.g., 'Curated A (12)')?
questions.guidelines: Yes: Active buttons have green highlight and correct challenge count. No: Missing highlight or incorrect count.

sections.name: Filter and Sort Panel in Content Window

questions.description: Does the collapsible filter/sort panel include controls for track (Design/Dev/QA), prize range, status (All/Completed/Active), and date range?
questions.guidelines: Yes: All controls are present and functional. No: One or more controls are missing.

questions.description: Do sort options (Track, Prize, Start Date) include ascending/descending toggles and update the list immediately?
questions.guidelines: Yes: Sort options are present with toggle and list updates instantly. No: Missing toggles or no immediate update.

questions.description: Does clearing all filters reset the view to default curation type?
questions.guidelines: Yes: Clearing all filters returns to the default curation type view. No: View remains in a filtered state.

questions.description: Does the challenge list display a scrollable, paginated list with current page, total pages, and navigation controls (Previous/Next/page numbers)?
questions.guidelines: Yes: All pagination elements are present and functional. No: Missing pagination or navigation controls.

questions.description: Does the UI show an empty state message when no challenges match filters and a loading spinner during data fetch?
questions.guidelines: Yes: Empty state and loading spinner are displayed correctly. No: Missing either or both states.

questions.description: Does the ChallengeCard component display challenge metadata (name, status, track, start date, prize, tags, description) and curation annotations (category, story points)?
questions.guidelines: Yes: All metadata and annotations are displayed correctly. No: Missing any required field.

questions.description: Does the description field truncate content with 'show more' if longer than 3 lines?
questions.guidelines: Yes: Description is truncated and 'show more' link appears. No: Description is not truncated or link is missing.

questions.description: Does the mock challenges.service.ts file provide stubbed API responses for challenge list and details with curation categories and story points?
questions.guidelines: Yes: Mock service returns data with curation and story points. No: Missing or incorrect mock data.

questions.description: Does Challenge.model.ts define TypeScript interfaces for Challenge and Curation Annotation data matching Topcoder v6 API model?
questions.guidelines: Yes: Interfaces include name, status, track, prize, startDate, tags, description, and curation properties (category, storyPoints). No: Missing or mismatched fields.

questions.description: Does the useChallenges.ts hook use SWR to fetch and manage challenge list data, handling loading, error, and revalidation states?
questions.guidelines: Yes: Hook correctly manages loading, error, and revalidation. No: Missing state handling or incorrect SWR usage.

questions.description: Does the useAdminRole.ts hook check if the user has administrator role and integrate with profileContext?
questions.guidelines: Yes: Hook returns boolean based on profileContext and administrator role. No: Hook is missing or does not use profileContext.

questions.description: Are new components organized under src/apps/rfp-tool/lib/components, models, services, hooks, and mock directories with index.ts files for importability?
questions.guidelines: Yes: All components are in correct directories with index.ts. No: Incorrect structure or missing index files.

questions.description: Are admin-related constants (curation types, track options, pagination defaults) defined in src/apps/rfp-tool/src/config/constants.ts and used throughout the admin UI?
questions.guidelines: Yes: Constants are defined and used correctly. No: Missing or unused constants.

questions.description: Does the UI support MOCK_ADMIN_MODE constant or environment variable to force admin tab enablement during development, bypassing actual role check?
questions.guidelines: Yes: Development mode enables admin tab regardless of role. No: Mode is missing or not functional.
